back_translation: Therefore, we must pay close attention to what we have heard, lest we drift away
Because if the word that was spoken by angels was firm, and every transgression and disobedience received just punishment
How shall we escape, if we neglect such a great salvation? This salvation, which the Lord first began to preach, was confirmed to us by those who heard Him
At the same time, God also testified along with them, by signs, wonders, various powers, and the giving of gifts of the Holy Spirit, according to the will of Him
Because God did not put the world to come under the rule of angels, the world that we are speaking about
But someone has testified somewhere, saying, ‘Who indeed is man, that you remember him, or who is the son of man, that you care for him?
You made him slightly lower than the angels, and crowned him with glory and honor
You made all things to be under his feet.’ When God put all things under his subjection, He did not leave anything outside of his control. But at present, we do not yet see all things subject to him

But we see Jesus, the One whom God made lower than the angels for a short time, receiving the crown of glory and honor, because of suffering even unto death, in order that He might taste death for every person, by the grace of God

Because it was fitting for God, the One for whom all things exist and through whom all things exist, in bringing many sons into glory, that He would make the pioneer of their salvation perfect through suffering
Because both the One who sanctifies and those who are sanctified come from the same source. Therefore, He is not ashamed to call them brothers
He says, ‘I will proclaim the name of yours to the brothers of mine. In the midst of the church, I will sing praise to you’
And again He says, ‘I will trust in Him.’ And again, ‘Behold, I and the children whom God has given to me’

Therefore, since the children have blood and flesh, He himself also shared in blood and flesh likewise, in order that through His death He might destroy the one who has power over death, namely the devil
And so that He might set those people free, namely those who were oppressed in slavery their whole lives because of fear of death
Because in fact, He does not help angels, but He helps the descendants of Abraham

Therefore, He had to be made like His brothers in every respect, in order that He might be a merciful and faithful high priest, in service toward God, so that He might atone for the sins of the people
Because in the matter that He himself suffered when He was tested, He is therefore able to help those who are being tested
